author: Pepijn Peeters
message: Use related key for BelongsToMany::touch() (#60708)

author: DAFFA ADITYA PRATAMA
message:
[13.x] Route incrementEachQuietly/decrementEachQuietly through Model::__call (#60737)

These two methods were added recently (#60720) as protected, but were
not included in the list of methods that Model::__call forwards to the
instance. Calling them dynamically on a model -- e.g.
\->incrementEachQuietly(['votes' => 1]) -- was therefore forwarded
to the query builder, which has no such method, and threw a
BadMethodCallException. Every sibling (increment, decrement,
incrementQuietly, decrementQuietly, incrementEach, decrementEach) is
already listed; this adds the two missing quiet-each variants.

message: Add test for the Email rule's strict method (#60740)

author: Sergey Sannikov
message:
[13.x] Fix Number::forHumans() and abbreviate() returning "-0" for tiny negative values (#60736)

* [13.x] Fix Number::forHumans() and abbreviate() returning "-0" for tiny negative values

For a negative number whose magnitude rounds down to zero at the requested
precision (e.g. Number::forHumans(-0.4)), summarize() prepended the sign
unconditionally and produced "-0". Only add the sign when the summarized
magnitude is not the zero representation.

* Update Number.php

---------

author: Lucas Michot
message: Fix stale transformations being reapplied when branching an Image after toBytes() (#60748)

author: Lucas Michot
message:
Fix Str::containsAll() returning true for an empty needles array (#60746)

Str::containsAll('...', []) returned true because the loop simply never ran, contradicting its own docblock and the contains()/endsWith()/startsWith() convention of returning false for an empty needles array.

author: Denys Finchenko
message: Add test for the Uri toStringable method (#60739)

author: Lucas Michot
message:
Replace manual all/any foreach loops with array_all/array_any (#60742)

Continues the pattern already used across the codebase (Arr::has, Gate::any, MessageBag, etc.) by replacing hand-rolled foreach-with-early-return loops with the array_all/array_any helpers where the collection being iterated is a plain array.

author: Lucas Michot
message:
Use PHPUnit rector sets instead of individual rules (#60744)

Skips the specific rules within each set that would otherwise change behavior, so this is a no-op for the current codebase.

author: Lucas Michot
message:
[13.x] Optimize by using contains or doesntContain  (#60745)

* Use Collection contains/doesntContain instead of filter/reject + count/isEmpty checks

Replaces map/filter/reject chains ending in count() or isEmpty()/isNotEmpty() checks, as well as an in_array() lookup, with the more direct contains()/doesntContain() methods.

* Use doesntContain instead of negated contains

author: Shane Rosenthal
message:
[13.x] Add incrementEachQuietly and decrementEachQuietly to Eloquent models (#60720)

Adds quiet variants of `incrementEach` and `decrementEach`, mirroring the
existing `incrementQuietly`/`decrementQuietly` methods for the single-column
`increment`/`decrement`. Closes a consistency gap where every other mutator
had a "Quietly" counterpart except the "Each" pair.

message:
Make MailFake::assertQueuedTimes() public (#60710)

It was left protected while the equivalent assertSentTimes() method
is public, so it can't be called directly as Mail::assertQueuedTimes(),
unlike its sent-mail counterpart.

author: nuno maduro
message:
[13.x] Adds first-party support for `image` processing (#59276)

This PR introduces Laravel’s first-party image manipulation API through a new Illuminate\Image component, backed by Intervention Image and supporting GD / Imagick drivers. Images may be read from uploads, storage disks, paths, URLs, bytes, or Base64 strings, then fluently transformed through an ordered pipeline with operations such as resize, scale, cover, contain, crop, rotate, orient, blur, grayscale, sharpen, and flipping.

The API also supports format conversion, quality control, optimization, inspection of processed image metadata, and convenient filesystem storage methods. Image drivers are managed through Laravel’s standard manager pattern, allowing applications to select drivers per image, configure a default driver, register custom drivers, and provide custom transformation handlers.
